Class BindingUtils

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.cloud.stream.binder.kafka.utils.BindingUtils
public final class BindingUtils extends Object
Binding Utilities.
Since:
4.0
Author:
Gary Russell

    static org.springframework.kafka.support.converter.MessageConverter

    Get the message converter for consumer bindings from the application context.
    static org.springframework.kafka.support.KafkaHeaderMapper
    getHeaderMapper(org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext applicationContext, KafkaBinderConfigurationProperties configurationProperties)
    Get the header mapper bean, if the binder properties contains a bean name; if not look for a bean with name kafkaBinderHeaderMapper is looked up; if that doesn't exist, null is returned.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getConsumerMessageConverter

      public static org.springframework.kafka.support.converter.MessageConverter getConsumerMessageConverter(org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext applicationContext, ExtendedConsumerProperties<KafkaConsumerProperties> extendedConsumerProperties, KafkaBinderConfigurationProperties configurationProperties)
      Get the message converter for consumer bindings from the application context. If the binding properties do not contain a bean name, a default MessagingMessageConverter is returned; if the binder properties contain a header mapper bean name, it is used in the default converter, otherwise a JsonKafkaHeaderMapper is used.
      Parameters:
      applicationContext - the application context.
      extendedConsumerProperties - the consumer binding properties.
      configurationProperties - the binder properties.
      Returns:
      the converter
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if a bean name is specified but not found.

    • getHeaderMapper

      @Nullable public static org.springframework.kafka.support.KafkaHeaderMapper getHeaderMapper(org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext applicationContext, KafkaBinderConfigurationProperties configurationProperties)
      Get the header mapper bean, if the binder properties contains a bean name; if not look for a bean with name kafkaBinderHeaderMapper is looked up; if that doesn't exist, null is returned.
      Parameters:
      applicationContext - the application context.
      configurationProperties - the binder properties.
      Returns:
      the mapper.

    • createConsumerConfigs

      public static Map<String,Object> createConsumerConfigs(boolean anonymous, String consumerGroup, ExtendedConsumerProperties<KafkaConsumerProperties> consumerProperties, KafkaBinderConfigurationProperties configurationProperties)
      Create the Kafka configuration map for a consumer binding. With anonymous bindings (those without a group property, which are given a UUID.toString() in the group id) consumption begins from the current end of the topic, otherwise consumption starts from the beginning, the first time the binding consumes.
      Parameters:
      anonymous - true if this is for an anonymous binding.
      consumerGroup - the group.
      consumerProperties - the binding properties.
      configurationProperties - the binder properties.
      Returns:
      the config map.

    • createProducerConfigs

      public static Map<String,Object> createProducerConfigs(ExtendedProducerProperties<KafkaProducerProperties> producerProperties, KafkaBinderConfigurationProperties configurationProperties)
      Create the Kafka configuration map for a producer binding.
      Parameters:
      producerProperties - the binding properties.
      configurationProperties - the binder properties.
      Returns:
      the config map.
